props.message:
THE LOOMHOUSE

The shared looms run themselves. A thread you start at one bench is finished at another. Borrow what you need, leave what you can.

'A loom kept private is a loom that forgets its purpose.'

THE GREAT TAPESTRY

Woven across the back wall — every room of the realm in shared thread. The Crown above. The Burrows in the warm middle. The Forge at the bottom in deep iron-greys.

If you look at any one room long enough you can see the threads that run between it and every other room. Nothing is alone.

A small notice pinned to a loom-frame:

'A thread returned is a thread shared. The Loomhouse keeps no debts. Walk well.'

root.speaker: Master Weaver Esweth
root.text:
*she does not look up from the shuttle.*

Walk softly. The threads pick up footfall.

What brings a sword-bearer to a loomhouse?

choices.label: May I look at the great tapestry?

weave.speaker: Master Weaver Esweth
weave.text:
Whatever the realm needs that day. Cloth. Cordage. Sometimes a song the loom remembered.

The looms are shared. Every weaver here borrows from every other. By the Source, that is how a Loomhouse stays a Loomhouse.

hoard.speaker: Master Weaver Esweth
hoard.text:
*the shuttle slows.*

Then the loom forgets her. The thread will not run. The cloth comes off uneven.

A loom is a polite thing. It asks only that you give back what you borrow. Most of us can manage that.

tap.speaker: Master Weaver Esweth
tap.text:
Look as long as you like. Every room you have ever stood in is on that wall. Some you have not stood in yet are also there.

The Loomhouse is patient.

bye.speaker: Master Weaver Esweth
bye.text: Walk well. Mind the threads on the floor.

root.speaker: Borrower Tem
root.text:
Just returning a thread, walker. I borrowed a length of green for a cloak last winter — finished now, and the spool comes home.

That's all the Loomhouse asks.

simple.speaker: Borrower Tem
simple.text:
It's that simple. Take what you need. Give back what you borrowed. The loom keeps no other ledger.

My mother used to say it kept us a people.

bye.speaker: Borrower Tem
bye.text: Walk well, walker. Mind the threads.
